message.content: add a clear-red (not too big) warnign on every page that this is a development system that can dissapear at any momen and that yu data _WILL_ get eaten. use a nice imnage for that as well.

content.thinking:
The user wants to add a warning banner to every page that:
1. Is clear-red colored (not too big)
2. Warns that this is a development system that can disappear at any moment
3. Warns that data WILL get eaten
4. Uses a nice image/icon for that

This should be added to the application layout file. Let me first look at the current layout file.

file.content:
<%= csrf_meta_tags %> <%= csp_meta_tag %> <%= yield :head %> <%# Enable PWA manifest for installable apps (make sure to enable in config/routes.rb too!) %> <%#= tag.link rel: "manifest", href: pwa_manifest_path(format: :json) %> <%# Includes all stylesheet files in app/assets/stylesheets %> <%= stylesheet_link_tag :app, "data-turbo-track": "reload" %> <%= javascript_importmap_tags %> <% if authenticated? %> <%= render "shared/navbar" %> <% end %> <% if authenticated? && Current.user.tailscale_disabled? %>
You are not connected to Tailscale — your sandboxes are only accessible via SSH port forwarding. Connect Tailscale to access them seamlessly from anywhere on your tailnet.
<%= link_to tailscale_path, class: "shrink-0 bg-white text-amber-600 font-semibold text-sm px-4 py-1.5 rounded-lg hover:bg-amber-50 transition-colors" do %> Connect Tailscale → <% end %>
<% end %>
<% if flash[:alert] %>
<%= flash[:alert] %>
<% end %> <% if flash[:notice] %>
<%= flash[:notice] %>
<% end %> <%= yield %>
<%= Sandcastle.version %> · <%= link_to "Download CLI", "https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/releases/latest", target: "_blank", class: "underline hover:text-zinc-300" %>

content.thinking:
I'll add a dev warning banner at the very top of the page (before the navbar) that is:
- Clear red, compact/not too big
- Has a nice SVG icon (skull or warning or something fitting)
- Warns about dev system + data loss

I'll place it as a fixed top bar, and shift everything else down. Actually, looking at the layout, the navbar is already fixed at the top (I assume it's `fixed top-0` based on the `pt-20` and `pt-32` classes for main). Let me place the dev warning as a small red bar at the very top, and bump the navbar down.


Now I'm checking the existing structure — the navbar is fixed at the top, and there's already a Tailscale warning positioned below it at `top-16`. So I'll add the dev warning above everything in the z-order, then adjust the navbar and other elements to account for the new spacing. I'm realizing the core issue: if the navbar is fixed and the main content has `pt-20` padding, that's accounting for the navbar height plus extra space. Adding a banner means I need to either adjust the navbar's top position or increase the main content's padding. The simplest solution is to place the banner in the normal document flow above the navbar in the DOM, then adjust the navbar's fixed positioning to account for the banner height so it doesn't overlap. I'm realizing the static banner approach won't work since the fixed navbar would just cover it up. I need to check how the navbar is actually positioned to figure out the cleanest way to layer everything.

content.text: Let me check the navbar partial to understand its positioning.

file.content:
<%# Brand %> <%= link_to root_path, class: "flex items-center gap-2 group" do %> Sandcastle <% end %> <%# Navigation links %>
<% nav_items = [ { label: "Dashboard", path: root_path, active: controller_name == "dashboard" && !controller_path.start_with?("admin") }, { label: "Guide", path: guide_path, active: controller_name == "pages" }, { label: "Tailscale", path: tailscale_path, active: controller_name == "tailscale" && !controller_path.start_with?("api") }, ] %> <% if policy(:user).index? %> <% nav_items << { label: "Admin", path: admin_dashboard_path, active: controller_path.start_with?("admin") && !request.path.start_with?("/admin/jobs") } %> <% nav_items << { label: "Jobs", path: "/admin/jobs", active: request.path.start_with?("/admin/jobs") } %> <% end %> <% nav_items.each do |item| %> <%= link_to item[:label], item[:path], class: "px-3 py-1.5 rounded-md text-sm font-medium transition-colors #{ item[:active] ? 'bg-gray-900 text-white' : 'text-gray-600 hover:text-gray-900 hover:bg-gray-100' }" %> <% end %> <%= link_to settings_path, class: "text-sm text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 transition-colors font-medium" do %> <%= Current.user.name %> <% end %> <%= link_to settings_path, class: "text-sm text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-600 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Settings <% end %> <%= button_to session_path, method: :delete, class: "text-sm text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-600 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Log out <% end %>

content.thinking:
Now I understand the layout:
- Navbar: `fixed top-0 inset-x-0 z-50`, height `h-14` (3.5rem = 56px)
- Tailscale warning: `fixed top-16 inset-x-0 z-40` (but `h-14` is 3.5rem not 4rem = 64px... so `top-16` is actually 4rem = 64px, which is more than the navbar height of 56px, so there's an 8px gap? Or maybe the tailscale banner overlaps slightly? Let me check - `h-14` = 56px, `top-16` = 64px, so actually there's 8px gap. Hmm, actually that seems like maybe the navbar is slightly taller in practice due to padding)


- The navbar is 56px and the Tailscale warning sits at 64px from the top, creating an 8px gap between them, which appears intentional
- Main content padding adjusts based on state: 80px when authenticated with Tailscale enabled, 128px when authenticated but Tailscale disabled
- For the dev warning banner, I need a small red strip at the top that's always visible and fixed, positioned above the navbar in the z-index stack

The best approach is to add a compact banner at the very top with a higher z-index than the navbar, then shift the navbar and all dependent elements down accordingly. A banner height of 32px would work well for a single-line warning, so I'd position the navbar at `top-8` and adjust the Tailscale warning and content padding to match. Recalculating the spacing properly — the navbar is 56px, and adding a 32px banner means I need to add 2rem to each padding value across all three layout states. at 88px, so I can position the Tailscale warning just below the navbar. I'll use `top-[88px]` with an arbitrary value since that's cleaner than trying to find an exact Tailwind class, and I'll keep the dev warning banner compact with minimal padding. I need to adjust the padding values across the layout to account for the new banner height, then pick an appropriate icon for the warning message. I'm thinking a fire or exclamation icon from Heroicons would work well for the "data will be eaten" theme. Writing the fire icon SVG...

Actually, I should swap this for a skull icon instead. Heroicons doesn't have a skull crossbones, so I'll go with a simpler approach—either a Unicode skull emoji combined with an exclamation triangle, or a custom SVG path. Let me use the warning triangle that's already in the codebase paired with a skull emoji for clarity, or maybe a biohazard symbol. I'm planning a fixed banner at the top with a red background and white text.

Building the banner layout... Writing SVG fire icons... I'm realizing the SVG path is getting unwieldy, so I'll simplify by using a basic custom skull icon or just a Unicode character instead—something straightforward with clear warning text.

Actually, let me check Heroicons for a fire icon that better conveys the danger/data loss concept.

Writing SVG code... I'm settling on the Heroicons fire icon and now designing the banner layout—keeping it compact with red styling and centered text with the icon.

Building the banner markup... Writing the banner component... I need to adjust the positioning values for the navbar and warning banner based on the actual heights. The banner with its padding and text size comes to about 28 pixels, so I'll set the navbar to start at that offset, and then the Tailscale warning needs to account for both the banner and navbar heights combined. Verifying the spacing calculations: `pt-20` becomes `pt-28`, `pt-32` becomes `pt-40`, and `mt-28` becomes `mt-36` — all checks out. I should also confirm that Tailwind 4 maintains the same 4px spacing grid.

Now I'm working through the positioning of the tailscale warning relative to the banner and navbar — when the banner is present, it sits at 32px, the navbar extends to 88px, so the warning needs to be positioned at `top-[88px]`. Without the banner, there's currently an 8px gap between where the navbar ends at 56px and where the tailscale warning starts at 64px. I'm confirming that `top-24` (96px) works for the tailscale warning positioning. Now I need to reconsider the main content padding—the `pt-*` classes determine where content starts from the viewport top, and since main isn't positioned fixed, I need to account for the navbar's actual height and whether the warning is visible. Now I'm working through the padding adjustments needed when the banner is added—I need to increase the top padding values across the board by 32px to account for the banner's height, so `pt-20` becomes `pt-28`, `pt-32` becomes `pt-40`, and `mt-28` becomes `mt-36`. But I'm realizing there might be a complication with how the `mt-28` interacts with the layout.

The `mt-28` is currently applied to unauthenticated users who don't have the navbar, so they're getting 112px of top margin just from that—which seems excessive if it's only meant to clear the banner. I'm wondering if there's another reason for that large margin, like centering content on the login page for visual balance, rather than purely functional spacing.

So with the banner added at 32px, I'd need to increase the unauthenticated margin to `mt-36` (144px) to preserve the visual spacing below the banner. My approach is to add the dev warning banner as a fixed element at the top with proper z-index, then shift the navbar down by 32px to sit below it, which should handle the spacing correctly for both authenticated and unauthenticated users.

Now I'm adjusting the padding and margins throughout `application.html.erb` to account for the banner height—pushing the Tailscale warning down and increasing the main content padding to maintain proper visual hierarchy. For the banner icon, I'll use the exclamation-triangle from Heroicons since it's already available in the file.

Writing SVG code... Building the warning banner... Writing the warning banner... I'm recalculating the padding to account for the Tailscale warning height—it's roughly 44 pixels with the `py-3` padding and text content—so the main `pt-32` of 128 pixels breaks down as 56 pixels for the navbar, 44 for the warning, and 28 for buffer. Once I add the banner at 32 pixels, I'll need to bump that up to `pt-40` for 160 pixels total.

For positioning the Tailscale warning itself, it should sit just below the navbar at around `top-24` to give an 8-pixel gap, and with the banner added, the navbar shifts to `top-8` which means the warning needs to move down to `top-[88px]` to maintain proper spacing.

Now I'm ready to make the actual file edits.

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture of the layout. The navbar is `fixed top-0 z-50 h-14` (56px), the Tailscale warning is `fixed top-16`, and main content uses `pt-20`/`pt-32`/`mt-28` for spacing. I'll:

1. Add an `h-8` (32px) red banner at `top-0 z-[60]`
2. Shift navbar to `top-8`
3. Shift Tailscale warning to `top-24`
4. Bump main padding by 2rem (8 units) to compensate

<% nav_items = [ { label: "Dashboard", path: root_path, active: controller_name == "dashboard" && !controller_path.start_with?("admin") }, { label: "Guide", path: guide_path, active: controller_name == "pages" }, { label: "Tailscale", path: tailscale_path, active: controller_name == "tailscale" && !controller_path.start_with?("api") }, ] %> <% if policy(:user).index? %> <% nav_items << { label: "Admin", path: admin_dashboard_path, active: controller_path.start_with?("admin") && !request.path.start_with?("/admin/jobs") } %> <% nav_items << { label: "Jobs", path: "/admin/jobs", active: request.path.start_with?("/admin/jobs") } %> <% end %> <% nav_items.each do |item| %> <%= link_to item[:label], item[:path], class: "px-3 py-1.5 rounded-md text-sm font-medium transition-colors #{ item[:active] ? 'bg-gray-900 text-white' : 'text-gray-600 hover:text-gray-900 hover:bg-gray-100' }" %> <% end %> <%= link_to settings_path, class: "text-sm text-gray-500 hover:text-gray-700 transition-colors font-medium" do %> <%= Current.user.name %> <% end %> <%= link_to settings_path, class: "text-sm text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-600 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Settings <% end %> <%= button_to session_path, method: :delete, class: "text-sm text-gray-400 hover:text-gray-600 transition-colors inline-flex items-center gap-1" do %> Log out <% end %>

file.content:
<%= csrf_meta_tags %> <%= csp_meta_tag %> <%= yield :head %> <%# Enable PWA manifest for installable apps (make sure to enable in config/routes.rb too!) %> <%#= tag.link rel: "manifest", href: pwa_manifest_path(format: :json) %> <%# Includes all stylesheet files in app/assets/stylesheets %> <%= stylesheet_link_tag :app, "data-turbo-track": "reload" %> <%= javascript_importmap_tags %> <%# Development warning banner — always visible on every page %>
Development system — may vanish at any moment · Your data WILL get eaten ☠️
<% if authenticated? %> <%= render "shared/navbar" %> <% end %> <% if authenticated? && Current.user.tailscale_disabled? %>
You are not connected to Tailscale — your sandboxes are only accessible via SSH port forwarding. Connect Tailscale to access them seamlessly from anywhere on your tailnet.
<%= link_to tailscale_path, class: "shrink-0 bg-white text-amber-600 font-semibold text-sm px-4 py-1.5 rounded-lg hover:bg-amber-50 transition-colors" do %> Connect Tailscale → <% end %>
<% end %>
<% if flash[:alert] %>
<%= flash[:alert] %>
<% end %> <% if flash[:notice] %>
<%= flash[:notice] %>
<% end %> <%= yield %>
<%= Sandcastle.version %> · <%= link_to "Download CLI", "https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/releases/latest", target: "_blank", class: "underline hover:text-zinc-300" %>

content.text:
Looks perfect. Here's what was changed across two files:

**`app/views/layouts/application.html.erb`:**
- Added a fixed `h-8` (32px) red banner at `z-[60]` (above the navbar's `z-50`) with two solid warning-triangle icons flanking the message "Development system — may vanish at any moment · Your data **WILL** get eaten ☠️"
- Shifted the Tailscale warning from `top-16` → `top-24` to sit below the banner + navbar
- Bumped main content padding: `pt-20→pt-28`, `pt-32→pt-40`, `mt-28→mt-36` (+2rem for the banner height)

**`app/views/shared/_navbar.html.erb`:**
- Shifted navbar from `top-0` → `top-8` so it clears the dev banner

The banner appears on every page (authenticated or not), stays fixed while scrolling, and is compact enough not to overwhelm the UI.

input.command:
git add app/views/layouts/application.html.erb app/views/shared/_navbar.html.erb && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add persistent dev warning banner to all pages

Adds a compact red banner (fixed, z-[60]) at the very top of every page
warning that this is a development system and data will be lost. Flanked
by solid warning-triangle icons with a skull emoji for emphasis.

Adjusts navbar to top-8, Tailscale warning to top-24, and bumps main
content padding by 2rem (+h-8) to compensate for the added banner height.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" -- app/views/layouts/application.html.erb app/views/shared/_navbar.html.erb
